message:
I've never been the person with a plan. My friends joke that I show up late with no snacks and a dead phone battery. But last month when a water main broke in our neighborhood, I actually *had* water stored. I found my old documents folder. My kids knew where to meet if we couldn't reach each other. I felt like a superhero, except the superpower was just... being a little bit ready.

It was such a weird, good feeling—not anxious, just steady. Like I'd finally done something for my family instead of always playing catch-up.

Has anyone else had that moment where being prepared actually paid off in a totally normal, non-dramatic way?
